archetypes.name: Observation -> Method
archetypes.description: A paper motivated by a concrete empirical or conceptual observation, followed by a method designed around that observation.
archetypes.rationale: Best when the idea begins with a failure mode, phenomenon, or insight.
archetypes.persuasion_strategy: Convince reviewers that the observation is real, non-trivial, and directly explains the method design.

sections.name: Introduction
sections.function: Build a problem-to-insight-to-method storyline.

sections.name: Related Work
sections.function: Position the observation against closest methods and broader background.

sections.name: Methodology
sections.function: Translate the key observation into a concrete algorithmic design.

sections.name: Experiments
sections.function: Show that the observation is valid and the method improves the target behavior.

archetypes.name: Theory -> Algorithm
archetypes.description: A paper deriving an algorithm from a formal property, bound, objective, or theoretical diagnosis.
archetypes.rationale: Best when the main contribution starts from a theorem, objective, or formal explanation.
archetypes.persuasion_strategy: Make the formal result useful by showing how it implies a better algorithm and measurable empirical gains.

sections.name: Introduction
sections.function: Connect a practical bottleneck to a formal gap.

sections.name: Preliminaries
sections.function: Define notation, assumptions, and the formal problem.

sections.name: Theory and Algorithm
sections.function: State the formal result and derive the algorithm.

sections.name: Experiments
sections.function: Validate both the theoretical implication and empirical competitiveness.

archetypes.name: Algorithm + Theoretical Analysis
archetypes.description: A method paper strengthened by analysis that explains when and why the algorithm works.
archetypes.rationale: Best when the method is primary but theory supports its credibility.
archetypes.persuasion_strategy: Balance practical improvements with enough theory to reduce the risk of heuristic engineering.

sections.name: Introduction
sections.function: Present an algorithmic bottleneck and preview formal support.

sections.name: Methodology
sections.function: Describe the algorithm as modular decisions tied to the claim.

sections.name: Theoretical Analysis
sections.function: Explain properties such as convergence, consistency, stability, or complexity.

sections.name: Experiments
sections.function: Prove practical value and isolate algorithmic components.

archetypes.name: Framework Unification
archetypes.description: A paper that unifies multiple methods, tasks, or assumptions under one general framework.
archetypes.rationale: Best when the value is a new perspective that explains several existing approaches.
archetypes.persuasion_strategy: Show that the framework simplifies prior work and enables new predictions, methods, or evaluations.

sections.name: Introduction
sections.function: Move from fragmented literature to a unifying view.

sections.name: Unified Framework
sections.function: Define the common representation and map prior work into it.

sections.name: Applications
sections.function: Demonstrate what the framework makes possible.

archetypes.name: Benchmark / Dataset Construction
archetypes.description: A paper proposing a dataset, benchmark, or evaluation protocol as the main contribution.
archetypes.rationale: Best when the primary artifact changes how a community measures progress.
archetypes.persuasion_strategy: Establish task importance, dataset quality, evaluation reliability, and new insights about model behavior.

sections.name: Introduction
sections.function: Argue that existing evaluation fails to measure an important capability.

sections.name: Benchmark Design
sections.function: Specify data source, annotation, metrics, and validation protocol.

sections.name: Evaluation
sections.function: Evaluate representative systems and derive community-facing findings.

archetypes.name: Empirical Analysis
archetypes.description: A paper whose main value is a systematic empirical study, diagnosis, or comparison.
archetypes.rationale: Best when the contribution is evidence and interpretation rather than a new method.
archetypes.persuasion_strategy: Make the experimental design comprehensive enough that the resulting claims are hard to dismiss.

sections.name: Introduction
sections.function: Explain why the field lacks reliable empirical understanding.

sections.name: Study Design
sections.function: Define questions, variables, models, datasets, and metrics.

sections.name: Results and Analysis
sections.function: Answer each research question with controlled evidence.

archetypes.name: Model Architecture Design
archetypes.description: A paper proposing a new model architecture or architectural component.
archetypes.rationale: Best when the main technical object is a model structure.
archetypes.persuasion_strategy: Tie every architectural choice to a failure mode and validate it through ablations.

sections.name: Introduction
sections.function: Move from model limitations to architectural design principles.

sections.name: Architecture
sections.function: Describe modules, data flow, objective, and complexity.

sections.name: Experiments
sections.function: Compare against strong architectures and isolate components.

archetypes.name: New Task Formulation
archetypes.description: A paper defining a new task, setting, or problem formulation.
archetypes.rationale: Best when the novelty lies in what should be solved, not only how to solve it.
archetypes.persuasion_strategy: Show that the task is important, not reducible to existing tasks, and measurable with clear protocols.

sections.name: Introduction
sections.function: Justify why the new task should exist.

sections.name: Task Definition
sections.function: Specify inputs, outputs, constraints, metrics, and assumptions.

sections.name: Baselines and Experiments
sections.function: Demonstrate feasibility and expose why existing methods struggle.

archetypes.name: Comparative Analysis
archetypes.description: A paper comparing families of methods to derive practical or scientific conclusions.
archetypes.rationale: Best when the contribution is a careful comparison that changes how methods are understood.
archetypes.persuasion_strategy: Avoid shallow benchmarking by making comparison axes explicit and claims conditional on evidence.

sections.name: Introduction
sections.function: Motivate why existing comparisons are incomplete or misleading.

sections.name: Comparison Framework
sections.function: Define method families, evaluation axes, and controlled variables.

sections.name: Comparative Results
sections.function: Report trade-offs and explain when each method works.
